name: Week 10 KB Full Body — Skill + Mobility (Form-First, Light)
description: First KB session back after W9 sick-week rest — and structurally lighter than past Tuesdays. **Five hard days a week is the road back to injury.** This week, KB Tuesday is reframed: skill + mobility + light grooving, NOT a fourth strength day. The heavy strength block from W8 is gone. C&P, rows, and goblet squats stay light (35lb max). Swings cut to half-volume. Spotlights: C&P (technique drilling) and TGU (left side weak link). Modern KB cues throughout. If anything feels heavy, you've gone too far — pull weight, slow down.

dayOverview.theme: Tuesday is now a skill + mobility day, not a strength day. Mon/Wed/Fri are the strength days; Tue/Thu are the recovery/skill flank. Form is the only progression on KB days.

spotlights.why: C&P at 50lb was a W7-W8 breakthrough lift, but it's also a complex technique. W10 drops to 35lb and drills the technique — knee-bent clean catch, hip drive, ribs-down lockout. Build the pattern, not the load.
Left side has been the weak link for weeks. W10 spotlights TGU checkpoints — every position gets a pause, left side gets the freshest reps. Stay at 25lb, no chase.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W10 C&P + TGU · W11 swing mechanics + bent press leverage · W12 windmill + cossack mobility · W13 cycle back to C&P + TGU. ~3 weeks per cycle.
watchpoints: **Re-acquaintance week — back to home base, not chasing PRs.** Bent press / cossack / windmill at 25 (W7 proven sweet spot). C&P starts at 35, caps at 40 today. **No 50 C&P** — going there cold after 3 weeks off is the same trap that triggered the W8 QL spasm. Climb the ladder 35→40→45→50 over the next 3-4 KB sessions.
QL/lower back on swings — even at 25lb, the hip-hinge-under-load pattern is the W8/W9 trigger. Cut the set if it talks.
5 hard days/week = injury territory. Tue/Thu should leave you fresher, not more cooked. The pushing days are Mon/Wed/Fri.

exercises.notes: Light bell (15-20lb), big slow circles. Loosen the shoulders before any overhead work.
**LOAD:** 25lb. Find your depth — same depth focus as gym squats. Sit in the bottom for a beat. NOT a working set, just mobility.

**LOAD:** 25lb (light, just for the hinge groove).
- **Hike pass** between the legs, NOT a squat-and-lift
- Lats packed down, bell stays close to the body
- Hips snap forward — bell floats to chest height on momentum
- Watch the QL even at this weight

sets.name: Banded Shoulder Prep (Scap Activation, ~3 min)

exercises.notes: Heels tucked, mini-band overhead, hands wider than shoulders. **Sustained tension** — primes scap upward rotation for bent press + windmill.
Pull band down behind head — **elbows drive to ribs**, scaps depress. Lat-pulldown pattern.
Band wraps the upper back. **Press out to T** — fires rear delts and mid-traps before C&P.
Band at sternum, **pull apart, scaps retract**, ribs down.
Sweep arms further back. **Don't overarch** the lower back to chase range.
15s each side. Half-windmill with band — **lat + side-body opener**, primes the bent press lateral hinge.
Both arms in V/Y overhead. **Hold and breathe** — lower traps fire here. Closes the prep.

sets.notes: 1 round of all 7 banded moves (~3 min). Scap + rear delt activation before the heavier KB skill work. Sustained tension throughout, no rest between positions.

sets.name: Block 1: B-Stance Flow

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 25lb (W7-W8 sweet spot, hold).
- B-stance: one foot back, **heel up** on the back foot — most weight on the front leg
- Hike pass between legs, lats engaged
- Hips snap forward, NOT lifted by the arms
**Catch into rack on the swing.** Soft elbow into the body — no slam. Flow should feel smooth, not violent.
Stay in B-stance, squat from rack. Front-loaded squat — chest tall, back foot heel up.
Punch overhead from rack. Lock out hard at the top — bicep by the ear, ribs down (no rib flare).
Bell locked overhead, step into lunge. **Bicep stays glued to the ear** — if it drifts forward, the lockout is fake. Long stride, vertical front shin.

sets.notes: 25lb. 3 reps each side x 3 rounds. Same volume as W7-W8 — proven. Don't add load.

sets.name: Block 2: Skill Work (Bent Press / Cossack / Windmill)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 25lb (back to home base — W3/W7 proven sweet spot). Drop to 20 if anything feels off after 3 weeks away.
- **Bell starts in rack, eye on bell throughout** — head turn is part of the lift
- **Lateral hip hinge AWAY from bell side** — knee soft on bell side
- **Spine stays long** — lateral hinge with overhead load, NOT a side bend
- Press up by *moving body down*, not by pushing bell up — the press is leverage
- Slow descent, controlled return to rack

**LOAD:** 25lb goblet (W7 sweet spot — best depth at this weight, don't chase heavier).
- Sit DEEP, heels stay down
- Working leg in full external rotation
- **Mobility, not strength** — heel popping up = drop to 20 or shorten stance

**LOAD:** 25lb (W7 home base). Drop to 20 if the hinge gets sketchy — long spine wins over load.
- Bell locked overhead, eye tracks bell
- **Hip hinge AWAY from bell side** (same as bent press)
- Reach free hand toward inside of front foot
- Long spine — stretch should be in the **hip flexor of the back leg**, NOT lower back

**LOAD:** 25lb (hold). Reps trimmed (2 not 3) — quality over quantity.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — every checkpoint gets a deliberate pause:**
- **Lying:** bell punched up, opposite leg straight, opposite arm 45° to body
- **Roll to elbow:** drive through opposite heel, eyes ON the bell
- **Roll to hand:** straight base arm, chest up
- **Hip bridge:** glute squeeze, ribs down
- **Sweep leg through to lunge:** plant the knee under the hip
- **Stand up:** drive through the front heel, bell stays vertical
- **Reverse:** same checkpoints in reverse, no rushing

**Left side first** — give it the freshest reps. The weak side gets the priority, not the leftovers.

**Reflection:** which checkpoint felt off on the left side? That's next week's drill.

**LOAD:** 35lb start, 40lb cap today. **No 50 today — that's the QL-spasm-territory weight after 3 weeks off, asking for the same injury back.** If 35×5 is invisible in round 1, jump to 40 for round 2 and stop there. The ramp 35 → 40 → 45 → 50 plays out over the next 3-4 KB sessions, not today.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every rep:**
- **Catch the clean with knees slightly bent** — absorbs impact, makes press seamless (W3 discovery, the cue that made 50 click)
- **Hips drive the clean**, NOT the arm pulling — feel the hinge, then the snap
- At rack: bell on the chest, **elbow tucked into ribs**, wrist neutral
- Press: **ribs DOWN** (no rib flare), bicep finishes by the ear, full lockout
- Lower with control to rack — no slam
- **Both sides feel symmetric?** That's the goal. Right has been ahead of left — make left match right's quality, not chase right's speed.

**Reflection:** does the knee-bent catch feel automatic? That cue should be invisible by now.
**LOAD:** 25lb. Light row, 2s pull / 2s lower. NOT a strength set — just enough to feel the back. Skip if shoulders are cooked from yesterday's push.
60s each side. Hip flexor release — same as legs day cooldown. Don't shortcut this.
60s. Adductors get tight on KB days from B-stance and cossacks.

tips: **RESTRUCTURED FROM W8** — Tuesday is a skill + mobility day now, NOT a strength day. The heavy strength block (50lb C&P/rows/goblet/swings) is gone.
**NEW W10:** Seated mini-band shoulder prep added after the warmup (@sculptherbody flow). 7 positions, ~3 min, sustained tension. Activates scaps + rear delts before bent press / TGU / C&P.
Heaviest bell touched today: 35lb (and that's the C&P spotlight — technique drilling at light load).
5 hard days/week = injury territory. Tue should leave you fresher than Mon, not more cooked.
Spotlights: TGU checkpoints (left side weak link) and C&P technique (knee-bent catch + ribs-down lockout).
Modern KB cues embedded — hike pass, B-stance heel up, lateral hinge bent press, checkpoint TGU, knee-bent C&P catch.
Cooldown is mandatory: pigeon + butterfly. Hips and adductors get tight on KB days — don't skip.
If anything feels heavy, the load is wrong for this day. Pull weight, slow down, or skip the block.
formPhilosophy: KB Tuesday is skill + mobility, not strength. Modern KB cues drilled at light loads — hip drive on swings, leverage on bent press, checkpoint discipline on TGU, knee-bent catch on C&P. Form is the entire progression. The strength days are Mon/Wed/Fri; this is the recovery flank.
